name: Traders addresses (economic operators hub)
pageTitle: Addresses - Import and export applications - GOV.UK
caption: Traders
heading: Addresses
bodyCopy: This is the sender of the consignment.
This is the receiver or buyer of the consignment being shipped or transported.
This is usually the same as the consignee. You can select a different person if needed.
Providing a false address is an act of fraud.
This is where the consignment will be sent for final unloading, and must be in Great Britain. If this is not the same as the consignee or importer, select a different address.

structure.text: This is the sender of the consignment.
Addresses | Name | Address | Country | Change link | Linus George Ltd | 101 Main St, Suite 2, Building 2, Springfield, 10000 | England | Change
This is the receiver or buyer of the consignment being shipped or transported.
Add a consignee (empty state) / address table + Change (populated)
This is usually the same as the consignee. You can select a different person if needed.
Warning Providing a false address is an act of fraud.
This is where the consignment will be sent for final unloading, and must be in Great Britain. If this is not the same as the consignee or importer, select a different address.
Save and review (variant — shown only when this hub is reached via the review page 'Change traders' amend flow; alternative primary to 'Save and continue')

govukComponents.usage: H1 "Addresses" (xl); four section H2s (m): Consignor or exporter, Consignee, Importer, Place of destination
Explanatory description under each section heading
Renders a populated economic operator's address (Name / Address / Country / Change link). One table per populated role (consignor always populated; consignee shown once added).
govukComponents.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 98 (consignor only) / action 111 (consignor + consignee)

govukComponents.usage: Fraud warning in the Place of destination section: "Providing a false address is an act of fraud."

Primary actions observed in the create flow: "Save and return to hub" (id=save-and-return-button) and "Save and continue" (id=button-save-and-continue). A third variant, "Save and review", replaces "Save and continue" when the hub is reached from the review page's 'Change traders' amend flow (inferred, not observed in this trace — page-objects/notification/TradersAddressesPage.ts:22-24 btnSaveAndReview; driven at workflows/notification/ched-p-workflows.ts:1340).

Add-operator links (Add a consignor or exporter / Add a consignee / Add an importer / Add a place of destination), Change links on populated tables (edit-consignor/edit-consignee), and "Cancel and return to hub"

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: button.link-button.button-margin-right (id=populate-importer name=populate_importer value=importer; id=populate-place-of-destination name=populate_place_of_destination value=place-of-destination)
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: A real form submit <button> styled to look like a text link, labelled "Same as consignee". Clicking POSTs to /traders and copies the consignee address into the importer / place-of-destination role. Appears only once a consignee has been added. Both roles carry their own 'Same as consignee' button and both are populated this way in the standard happy-path flow (workflows/notification/ched-p-workflows.ts:414-416 clicks #populate-importer first, then the role-named 'Same as consignee' button which — now that the importer button is consumed — uniquely resolves to the place-of-destination one).
nonStandardPatterns.concern: govuk-frontend has no "link styled as button" component; this is bespoke CSS (link-button). It is a state-changing POST, so presenting it as a link is misleading for keyboard/AT users and outside the toolbox. Two buttons share the identical accessible name "Same as consignee" (disambiguated only by id), which is itself an accessibility smell.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: govuk-button with the govuk-button--secondary modifier (a secondary Button), keeping it as a form submit, with a distinct accessible name per role (e.g. "Importer same as consignee"). The new app should model "same as consignee" as an explicit secondary button or a radio-driven copy choice.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 111 (button inventory); corroborated by page-objects/notification/TradersAddressesPage.ts:14-16,26-32 and workflows/notification/ched-p-workflows.ts:414-416

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: table.trader-table.table-responsive with th/td classes col-name, col-address, col-country, col-edit, text-align-right
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Custom layout/responsive styling layered on top of govuk-table to lay out the Name / Address / Country / Change columns and right-align the Change link.
nonStandardPatterns.concern:
Bespoke responsive-table and column-width classes are outside govuk-frontend. Note: 'table-responsive' is a wrapping
around the table, not a class on the itself (the table carries govuk-table govuk-!-margin-bottom-2 trader-table). The whole table also has a govuk-visually-hidden , and the Change-column header is a govuk-visually-hidden Change link — i.e. screen-reader-only, NOT a visible column header (it only appears in innerText because govuk-visually-hidden clips rather than display:none).
Addresses
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: GOV.UK Summary list (govuk-summary-list) with an actions column is the idiomatic pattern for showing a captured entity with a Change action, and removes the need for custom table CSS. Alternatively a plain govuk-table with responsive utilities.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 98/111 (class inventory: col-address, col-country, col-edit, col-name, table-responsive, text-align-right, trader-table)

fields.name: add-consignor / edit-consignor
fields.label: Consignor or exporter
fields.hint: This is the sender of the consignment.
fields.control: navigation (add-link in empty state / address-table + Change-link in populated state — not a text input)
observedValues: Linus George Ltd | 101 Main St, Suite 2, Building 2, Springfield, 10000 | England (DEFAULT_CONFIG.consignorAddress, workflows/notification/ched-p-workflows.ts:149-159)
fields.conditionalOn: Always present; consignor is pre-populated at the observed action 98 (Linus George Ltd) and shown as an address table with a Change link (id=edit-consignor -> /traders/consignor/search). Empty state shows the link "Add a consignor or exporter" (id=add-consignor -> /traders/consignor/search) which routes into the consignor search+create sub-journey (SearchExistingConsignorOrExporter -> ConsignorCreation -> ConsignorConfirmation, workflows/notification/ched-p-workflows.ts:388-398).
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 98 (populated table + Change link id=edit-consignor); empty-state link text 'Add a consignor or exporter' (id=add-consignor, href .../traders/consignor/search) directly observed at action 85 of the SAME trace (earlier hub visit before consignor was added); corroborated by page-objects/notification/TradersAddressesPage.ts:6-8 (linkAddConsignorOrExporter) and workflows/notification/ched-p-workflows.ts:388

fields.name: add-consignee / edit-consignee
fields.label: Consignee
fields.hint: This is the receiver or buyer of the consignment being shipped or transported.
fields.control: navigation (add-link in empty state / address-table + Change-link in populated state — not a text input)
observedValues: Global Corp | 102 High St, Suite 3, Building 7, Riverdale, 10001 | England (DEFAULT_CONFIG.consigneeAddress, workflows/notification/ched-p-workflows.ts:160-170)
fields.conditionalOn: Empty state shows link "Add a consignee" (id=add-consignee); once added shows an address table with Change link. Adding a consignee is what makes the "Same as consignee" populate buttons appear on Importer and Place of destination. The Add link routes into the consignee search+create sub-journey (SearchExistingConsignee -> ConsigneeCreation -> ConsigneeConfirmation, workflows/notification/ched-p-workflows.ts:401-411).
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 98 (empty, Add a consignee) / action 111 (populated, Global Corp + Change); corroborated by page-objects/notification/TradersAddressesPage.ts:10-12 and workflows/notification/ched-p-workflows.ts:401

fields.name: populate_importer / add-importer / edit-importer
fields.label: Importer
fields.hint: This is usually the same as the consignee. You can select a different person if needed.
fields.control: navigation ('Same as consignee' submit button once a consignee exists / 'Add an importer' link — not a text input)
fields.conditionalOn: The "Same as consignee" populate button is shown only after a consignee has been added (absent at action 98, present at action 111). Otherwise only "Add an importer" is offered. In the standard flow the importer is always populated via 'Same as consignee' (#populate-importer), never via the Add link.
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 98 (only Add an importer) / action 111 (Same as consignee + Add an importer); corroborated by page-objects/notification/TradersAddressesPage.ts:26-28 (locatorPopulateImporter '#populate-importer') and workflows/notification/ched-p-workflows.ts:414

fields.name: populate_place_of_destination / add-place-of-destination
fields.label: Place of destination
fields.hint: This is where the consignment will be sent for final unloading, and must be in Great Britain. If this is not the same as the consignee or importer, select a different address.
fields.control: navigation ('Same as consignee' submit button once a consignee exists / 'Add a place of destination' link — not a text input)
fields.conditionalOn: Carries a Warning-text: "Providing a false address is an act of fraud." The "Same as consignee" populate button is shown only after a consignee has been added (absent at action 98, present at action 111). Must be in Great Britain. In the standard flow this role is also populated via 'Same as consignee' (workflows/notification/ched-p-workflows.ts:415 — the role-named 'Same as consignee' button resolves here once the importer button has been consumed).
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 98 (only Add a place of destination) / action 111 (Same as consignee + Add a place of destination); corroborated by page-objects/notification/TradersAddressesPage.ts:30-32 (btnPlaceOfDestinationSameAsConsignee '#populate-place-of-destination')

openQuestions: URL pattern in the page brief was /traders-addresses; the real observed path is /notification/vnet/protected/notifications/{chedReference}/traders (spec url corrected accordingly).

This is a hub, not a form: it has no free-text/select inputs of its own. The only real form controls are the two "Same as consignee" submit buttons (populate_importer, populate_place_of_destination) and Save and return / Save and continue. Add/Change links navigate to per-operator search+select sub-journeys (consignor/consignee/importer/final-destination /search) which are separate pages not mined here.

No validation error state was captured for this hub in this trace (0 errors), and NEITHER the page object (page-objects/notification/TradersAddressesPage.ts) NOR any CHED-P test or the accessibility test (tests/accessibility/ched-p-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:123-181) asserts any error message or any mandatory-operator rule on this hub — every test drives the happy path and always populates consignor + consignee + importer + place of destination before Save and continue. So whether Save and continue enforces mandatory operators (e.g. consignee / place of destination required) is a genuine GAP for a human to confirm; the happy-path habit is a weak signal that all four are expected but is not an asserted rule.

RESOLVED: Empty-state copy for the consignor section is "Add a consignor or exporter" (link id=add-consignor -> /traders/consignor/search). This IS directly observed in a trace — action 85 of trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d clicks this link on the hub while the consignor slot is still empty (confidence: confirmed, not merely inferred from the page object as an earlier draft stated).
The 'Change link' column header text and the visually-hidden usage on the trader-table need checking against the template for accessibility; consider a Summary list in the rebuild.

Variant primary button 'Save and review' (page-objects/notification/TradersAddressesPage.ts:22-24 btnSaveAndReview) replaces 'Save and continue' when this hub is reached from the review page's 'Change traders' amend flow (workflows/notification/ched-p-workflows.ts:1338-1340). Not observed in this create-flow trace — inferred. A human should confirm the button label and that it is a straight swap for 'Save and continue' in amend mode.

The shared page object also exposes btnSelect (getByRole('button', {name:'Select'}), page-objects/notification/TradersAddressesPage.ts:34-36), but it is used ONLY by the CHED-A journey (workflows/notification/ched-a-workflows.ts:353-355,1008-1010) — CHED-A presents pre-existing addresses with 'Select' buttons rather than add/search links. It is NOT part of the CHED-P/IUU rendering and no CHED-P trace or test exercises it; out of scope for IUU, noted only to explain a page-object member that does not apply here.

Both 'Same as consignee' buttons (importer and place of destination) share the identical accessible name and are only distinguishable by id — a recorded accessibility concern for the rebuild (see nonStandardPatterns).
